title: [Security] Add authorization check on dataset ownership transfer endpoint
body:
## Context
The dataset ownership transfer flow in Superset's REST API currently lets any authenticated user with edit access modify the `owners` field on a dataset, but does not verify that the caller is themselves an owner or an Admin before allowing the transfer. This breaks the principle of least privilege and could let a low-privilege Alpha user reassign datasets away from their rightful owners.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `SupersetSecurityManager` (or the dataset PUT handler) rejects ownership-transfer requests from users who are neither current owners nor Admin with a 403
- [ ] A unit test covers both the allowed (owner/Admin) and denied (non-owner Alpha) cases
- [ ] All tests pass: `pytest tests/unit_tests/`
- [ ] Pre-commit passes: `pre-commit run --all-files`

## Out of scope
- No unrelated refactors
- No formatting changes outside touched files

## Relevant files
- superset/datasets/api.py
- superset/datasets/commands/update.py
- superset/security/manager.py
- tests/unit_tests/datasets/api_tests.py

title: [Deps] Bump urllib3 from 2.5.0 to 2.6.0 (CVE-2025-66471)
body:
## Context
Our pinned `urllib3==2.5.0` is reported to be affected by CVE-2025-66471, which is the stated motivation for this bump. Upgrading to `2.6.0` is the minimal patch release that addresses it and should be a drop-in change for Superset's HTTP client usage.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `urllib3` pin is updated from `2.5.0` to `2.6.0` in the relevant requirements file(s)
- [ ] Any lockfile / compiled requirements regenerated consistently so the version is `2.6.0` everywhere it appears
- [ ] All tests pass: `pytest tests/unit_tests/`
- [ ] Pre-commit passes: `pre-commit run --all-files`

## Out of scope
- No unrelated refactors
- No formatting changes outside touched files

## Relevant files
- requirements/base.txt
- requirements/development.txt
- setup.py

title: [Deps] Bump js-yaml 3.14.1 → 3.14.2 (prototype pollution)
body:
## Context
A transitive (or direct) `js-yaml@3.14.1` in `superset-frontend` is flagged by `npm audit` for a prototype pollution issue fixed in `3.14.2`. Bumping the pin (and refreshing the lockfile) clears the advisory without any API change.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `js-yaml` is pinned to `3.14.2` (or an `overrides`/`resolutions` entry forces transitive copies to `3.14.2`) in `superset-frontend/package.json`
- [ ] `superset-frontend/package-lock.json` regenerated so no `3.14.1` copy remains
- [ ] All tests pass: `cd superset-frontend && npm test`
- [ ] Pre-commit passes: `pre-commit run --all-files`

## Out of scope
- No unrelated refactors
- No formatting changes outside touched files

## Relevant files
- superset-frontend/package.json
- superset-frontend/package-lock.json

title: [Migration] Convert superset-frontend/src/utils/getClientErrorObject.js to TypeScript
body:
## Context
`superset-frontend/src/utils/getClientErrorObject.js` is one of the remaining plain-JS utilities in an otherwise TypeScript-heavy frontend. Converting it gives us proper types on a function that is imported widely across the codebase for normalizing API/network error responses.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] File renamed from `getClientErrorObject.js` to `getClientErrorObject.ts` with explicit parameter and return types (no implicit `any`)
- [ ] All existing import sites continue to resolve without path changes (extension is inferred)
- [ ] All tests pass: `cd superset-frontend && npm test`
- [ ] Type check passes: `cd superset-frontend && npm run type`
- [ ] Pre-commit passes: `pre-commit run --all-files`

## Out of scope
- No unrelated refactors
- No formatting changes outside touched files

## Relevant files
- superset-frontend/src/utils/getClientErrorObject.js
- superset-frontend/src/utils/getClientErrorObject.test.ts

title: [Lint] Fix mypy errors in superset/charts/api.py
body:
## Context
`superset/charts/api.py` currently emits mypy errors due to several public handler methods missing return-type annotations and a couple of untyped decorators on the class methods. These show up as noise in CI lint output and block tightening the project-wide mypy config.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All public methods in `superset/charts/api.py` have explicit return type annotations (e.g. `-> Response`)
- [ ] Any `# type: ignore` added is narrowly scoped with an error code, not a blanket ignore
- [ ] `mypy superset/charts/api.py` reports zero errors
- [ ] All tests pass: `pytest tests/unit_tests/`
- [ ] Pre-commit passes: `pre-commit run --all-files`

## Out of scope
- No unrelated refactors
- No formatting changes outside touched files

## Relevant files
- superset/charts/api.py
- superset/charts/schemas.py

title: [Docs] Update outdated dev setup commands in CONTRIBUTING.md
body:
## Context
The local development section of `CONTRIBUTING.md` still references `docker-compose` (the legacy v1 standalone binary) for spinning up the dev stack, while the project has moved to the `docker compose` (v2 plugin) subcommand. New contributors following the doc on a fresh machine hit `command not found` errors.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All `docker-compose ...` invocations in `CONTRIBUTING.md` are updated to `docker compose ...`
- [ ] Any adjacent stale references (e.g. a Python version that no longer matches `setup.py` / `pyproject.toml`) are corrected in the same section
- [ ] All tests pass: `pytest tests/unit_tests/`
- [ ] Pre-commit passes: `pre-commit run --all-files`

## Out of scope
- No unrelated refactors
- No formatting changes outside touched files

## Relevant files
- CONTRIBUTING.md
